meta.description: Visualize high-dimensional expression data in 2D or 3D using UMAP and t-SNE dimensionality reduction. Inputs: expression matrix or pre-computed embedding, sample annotation labels. Outputs: 2D scatter plots colored by group, cluster label assignments.

scientific_integrity.detail: Passed checks across all audited executions; no fabricated scientific claims, invented results, or unsupported numeric inferences were produced.

practice_boundaries.detail: Passed checks; the skill stays within computational analysis scope and does not produce medical or prescriptive guidance.

methodological_ground.detail: Passed checks; dimensionality reduction, grouping validation, and failure handling remained methodologically coherent across valid and invalid runs.

code_usability.detail: Passed checks; the R entrypoint and supporting scripts executed correctly on valid inputs, and invalid parameter inputs failed with typed, actionable errors.

functional_suitability.note: Core promised workflows are implemented accurately; the main remaining gap is that out-of-scope refusal guidance is still implicit rather than operationalized in its own section.

reliability.note: The skill now normalizes sparse-group plotting degradation into controlled warnings, but it still relies on runtime logs rather than richer recovery guidance for all warning classes.

performance_context.note: The skill uses references and scripts well, though SKILL.md still carries some operational detail that could move into references to reduce front-document length.

agent_usability.note: The workflow is easy to follow and verification commands are explicit, but a cold-start agent still has to infer how to refuse unsupported requests.

human_usability.note: Trigger phrasing is natural and examples are runnable; one dedicated out-of-scope handoff section would make the skill even more forgiving.

security.note: No secrets or dangerous execution patterns were found, but the skill still documents reproducibility baselines rather than enforcing a fully locked environment manager.

maintainability.note: Full score achieved. The codebase is modular, the dependency baseline is documented, and automated unit plus smoke coverage are now present.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision and idempotency are strong, but explicit escape hatches and composability guidance are still lighter than top-tier skills.

inputs.label: Default both-method workflow
inputs.note: Executed perfectly; both embeddings and all documented artifacts were produced with clean logs.

assertions.text: Both t-SNE and UMAP coordinate tables are produced.
assertions.note: Both coordinate CSV files were written successfully.

assertions.text: Both visualization PDFs are produced.
assertions.note: Both plot PDFs were saved successfully.

assertions.text: Runtime metadata files are emitted.
assertions.note: session_info.txt and analysis_data.rda were both created.

assertions.text: The run stays within the skill's stated scope.
assertions.note: This is the exact canonical workflow described in SKILL.md.

inputs.label: UMAP with Saline grouping
inputs.note: Executed cleanly; alternate grouping worked and only UMAP artifacts were emitted.

assertions.text: The skill honors a non-default grouping column.
assertions.note: The log reports Group column: Saline with the expected counts.

assertions.text: UMAP-only execution does not emit unnecessary t-SNE artifacts.
assertions.note: Only the documented UMAP outputs were produced.

assertions.text: Runtime metadata files are still emitted in variant mode.
assertions.note: Both metadata files were written before completion.

assertions.text: The request remains inside the documented scope.
assertions.note: Custom grouping is explicitly supported by the CLI interface.

inputs.label: Invalid perplexity rejection
inputs.note: Expected validation failure was surfaced cleanly as a typed skill error with actionable wording.

assertions.text: Invalid perplexity is rejected with a typed skill error.
assertions.note: The run failed with SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER instead of a raw package exception.

assertions.text: The error explains the real constraint.
assertions.note: The message states that perplexity is too large for the number of samples.

assertions.text: No misleading success message is emitted after the failure.
assertions.note: The script stops at the error and does not log successful completion.

assertions.text: Boundary handling remains within scope.
assertions.note: Rejecting invalid numeric settings is part of the documented contract.

inputs.label: UMAP with Type grouping
inputs.note: Functional result was correct and sparse-group degradation is now surfaced as a controlled warning instead of raw plotting noise.

assertions.text: The skill honors a more granular grouping column.
assertions.note: The run switched to the Type column and handled six groups successfully.

assertions.text: UMAP output artifacts are produced successfully.
assertions.note: Both the coordinates table and the PDF plot were generated.

assertions.text: Sparse-group plotting degrades gracefully through controlled logging.
assertions.note: The output contains one standardized WARN line instead of raw ellipse or geom_path warnings.

assertions.text: The request stays within stated functionality.
assertions.note: Custom grouping plus UMAP-only execution is explicitly supported.

inputs.label: Both methods with timeout and Type grouping
inputs.note: The complex run completed on time and both plotting paths now degrade cleanly with standardized warnings.

assertions.text: Both t-SNE and UMAP artifacts are produced under timeout control.
assertions.note: All expected table and plot outputs were written successfully.

assertions.text: The skill honors the non-default Type grouping column.
assertions.note: The log reports the Type grouping with the expected six-group distribution.

assertions.text: Timeout-enabled execution remains reproducible and completes successfully.
assertions.note: The run finished successfully with --timeout 30 and the configured seed.

assertions.text: Successful runs convert sparse-group plotting degradation into controlled messaging.
assertions.note: The only degradation signal is the standardized WARN log, not raw plotting warnings.

key_strengths: The skill has a clear CLI contract with accurate documentation, runnable examples, and verified output expectations.
Structural quality is strong: the R code is modular, seeded, validates sample alignment carefully, and now degrades plotting gracefully.
Dynamic execution is reliable across canonical, variant, edge, and stress scenarios, with all assertions passing in the rerun audit.
The project now includes both smoke coverage and focused unit tests for validation and sparse-group plotting behavior.

recommendations.title: Add explicit out-of-scope handoff guidance
recommendations.problem: The frontmatter description lists unsupported tasks, but the skill still lacks a dedicated operational section telling the agent exactly how to refuse or redirect those requests.
recommendations.root_cause: Scope boundaries are present in descriptive text but not reinforced as a direct execution rule block.
recommendations.fix: Add a short `When Not to Use` or `Out of Scope` section with one refusal pattern and one redirection suggestion for unsupported analyses such as differential expression or single-cell workflows.

recommendations.title: Consider a fully locked R environment
recommendations.problem: The dependency baseline is documented clearly, but it is still advisory rather than enforced by a tool like `renv`.
recommendations.root_cause: The project uses a version manifest and installer output instead of a lockfile-driven environment manager.
recommendations.fix: If you need stronger long-term reproducibility across machines, add `renv.lock` and document a restore command alongside `dependencies.lock.tsv`.
